conditions: Chronic back pain (lumbar degenerative disc disease)
medications: Naproxen 500 mg as needed for back pain
Cyclobenzaprine 5 mg at night as needed (rare use)
health.mental_health: Mostly functional but under sustained stress from retirement planning and selling the business; intermittent insomnia and irritability when back pain flares. Avoids formal therapy due to time and stigma concerns.
recent_visits: Primary care visit for blood pressure follow-up and medication refills (Jan 2026)
Endocrinology lab review for TSH adjustment (Dec 2025)
Physical therapy evaluation for low back pain and core strengthening plan (Feb 2026)
Annual eye exam with discussion of hypertension-related risks (Nov 2025)

finance.debt: Owes roughly $160,000 on an SBA-backed business loan for equipment and build-out; also carries a small balance on a business line of credit used for flour/butter purchases during high-volume weeks. Working to pay both down before listing the bakery for sale.
investments: High-yield savings account for taxes and emergency fund
529 plan for a younger child/niece he helps support (limited contributions)
Life insurance cash value policy (considering surrender vs. keep)
recent_transactions: Paid quarterly estimated taxes to the IRS and California FTB (Jan 2026)
Purchased a replacement convection oven fan motor and service call ($1,480) (Feb 2026)
Retainer payment to a business broker to discuss a sale within 12–18 months ($3,500) (Dec 2025)
Paid medical bills for physical therapy co-pays and imaging deductible (Feb 2026)

relationships: Partner: Elena Mendez (long-term partner; helps with front-of-house on weekends)
Adult child: Lucas Larsson (lives in Long Beach; encourages him to retire sooner)
Sister: Karin Larsson (lives in San Diego; occasional tension about family obligations)
Close friend: Minh Tran (neighbor and fellow small business owner; sounding board for sale plans)
Key employee: Jasmine Patel (head baker; potential successor/manager after sale)
social.social_media_activity: Maintains a bakery Instagram and Facebook page with daily pastry posts and holiday pre-order announcements; personal accounts are minimal and mostly private. Avoids posting about health or retirement timing to prevent rumors among staff and customers.
private_conversations: Discussing whether to sell the bakery outright vs. keep partial ownership for steady income
Concerns about chronic back pain affecting ability to keep up with early-morning production
Negotiating with Elena about living arrangements and budget after retirement
Quiet worries about needing to help support a family member financially without telling others

schedule.work_hours: Typically Tue–Sun, 4:30am–2:30pm; admin/bookkeeping 2–3 evenings per week. Mondays are used for ordering, maintenance, and occasional rest.
regular_appointments: Blood pressure check-in with primary care every 3–4 months
Physical therapy once weekly (currently a 6-week block)
Monthly meeting with bookkeeper to reconcile payroll and sales tax
Weekly wholesale delivery route to two local coffee shops (early mornings)
upcoming_events: Meeting with CPA to model retirement income and tax impact of a business sale (Mar 2026)
Intro call with business broker to review valuation comps and timeline (Mar 2026)
Back follow-up visit if PT doesn’t reduce pain (Apr 2026)
Family weekend in San Diego for sister’s 60th birthday (May 2026)
schedule.daily_routine: Up around 3:45am, opens the bakery before dawn, rotates between mixing, proofing, and oven work; quick lunch mid-day; afternoons are customer-facing and inventory; evenings include stretching/heat for his back, brief bookkeeping, then early bed when possible.

professional.company: Harborlight Bakehouse LLC (independent neighborhood bakery in Los Angeles with a small wholesale arm)
projects: Preparing the bakery for sale within 12–18 months (organizing financials, SOPs, vendor contracts)
Training Jasmine Patel to handle ordering, scheduling, and quality control with less oversight
Refreshing the menu to improve margins (reducing low-profit items, standardizing portion sizes)
Negotiating a new flour contract and evaluating butter suppliers due to price volatility
Implementing a new POS/inventory system to make the business more turnkey for buyers
professional.performance_issues: Back pain and fatigue have led to occasional missed early shifts and shorter on-the-floor time; he is delegating more but worries about consistency and customer experience during the transition. He is also cautious about staff learning he plans to sell, fearing turnover before the sale.
workplace_relationships: Relies heavily on Jasmine Patel; trying to keep her motivated with a raise and a possible bonus tied to transition
Tense relationship with one part-time baker who resists new portion controls and recipe standardization
Friendly rapport with long-time vendors, but he is pushing harder on pricing, which has strained negotiations
Customer-facing relationship with regulars who ask about ‘what’s next’—he deflects to avoid premature disclosure

hobbies: Early-morning walks at the beach when his schedule allows (Santa Monica/Venice area)
Home espresso brewing and experimenting with sourdough hydration ratios
Listening to baseball radio broadcasts and attending a few Dodgers games per season
Weekend drives up the coast to decompress
habits: Tracks blood pressure at home a few times per week
Uses a heating pad and stretching routine nightly for back pain
Drinks 2–3 coffees most mornings; tries to cut off caffeine by noon
Occasional late-night snacking on leftover pastries when stressed (trying to stop for cholesterol and weight)
Keeps a folder of retirement paperwork and checks it obsessively during slow business weeks
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives with his partner in a rented two-bedroom apartment on the Westside; considering downsizing or relocating slightly inland after selling the bakery to reduce costs and commute. Prefers to keep plans quiet until the sale is more certain.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Enjoys bread and pastries but is trying to follow a lower-sodium, heart-conscious diet: more grilled fish/chicken, vegetables, and smaller portions. Limits alcohol to an occasional beer; avoids heavy late meals because of GERD.
